app.name: E-Commerce Platform
app.description: Customer-facing e-commerce web app with payment processing.

components.name: API Gateway
components.description: REST API gateway — all client traffic enters here

components.name: Auth Service
components.description: Handles login, registration, MFA, session management

components.name: PostgreSQL Database
components.description: Primary relational database — users, orders, products

components.name: Redis Cache
components.description: Session cache and rate limit counters

threats.title: Injection Attack — Web App
threats.description: Malicious input reaches Web App without proper validation or parameterisation, enabling SQL injection, command injection, or XSS.
threats.attack_vector: Crafted HTTP requests targeting form fields, query params, JSON bodies, headers.
threats.business_impact: Complete database exfiltration, remote code execution, customer data theft. SQLi breaches average $3.86M (IBM Cost of a Data Breach).
mitigations: Parameterise all database queries — never concatenate user input.
Implement strict input validation and allowlisting at every entry point.
Deploy WAF rules for SQLi, XSS, command injection patterns.
Use Content Security Policy headers to mitigate XSS impact.
Run SAST (Semgrep/Bandit) and DAST (ZAP) in CI pipeline.

threats.title: Injection Attack — API Gateway

threats.attack_vector: Crafted HTTP requests targeting form fields, query params, JSON bodies, headers.
threats.business_impact: Complete database exfiltration, remote code execution, customer data theft. SQLi breaches average $3.86M (IBM Cost of a Data Breach).

Deploy WAF rules for SQLi, XSS, command injection patterns.
Use Content Security Policy headers to mitigate XSS impact.
Run SAST (Semgrep/Bandit) and DAST (ZAP) in CI pipeline.

threats.title: Identity Spoofing — Web App
threats.description: Attacker impersonates a legitimate user or service accessing Web App via credential theft, session hijacking, or weak authentication bypass.
threats.attack_vector: Credential stuffing, brute-force, stolen session tokens, OAuth token replay.
threats.business_impact: Unauthorised access to customer accounts or internal systems. Potential for data theft, fraudulent transactions, reputational damage, and regulatory liability under GDPR/PCI-DSS.
mitigations: Enforce MFA on all user-facing authentication flows.
Implement account lockout after 5 failed attempts.
Use short-lived JWTs with refresh token rotation.
Monitor for credential stuffing patterns (IP velocity, geo-anomalies).

threats.title: Identity Spoofing — API Gateway
threats.description: Attacker impersonates a legitimate user or service accessing API Gateway via credential theft, session hijacking, or weak authentication bypass.
threats.attack_vector: Credential stuffing, brute-force, stolen session tokens, OAuth token replay.
threats.business_impact: Unauthorised access to customer accounts or internal systems. Potential for data theft, fraudulent transactions, reputational damage, and regulatory liability under GDPR/PCI-DSS.
mitigations: Enforce MFA on all user-facing authentication flows.
Implement account lockout after 5 failed attempts.
Use short-lived JWTs with refresh token rotation.
Monitor for credential stuffing patterns (IP velocity, geo-anomalies).

threats.title: Broken Access Control — Web App
threats.description: Insufficient authorisation checks in Web App allow a low-privilege user to access or modify resources belonging to other users or perform admin actions.
threats.attack_vector: Horizontal privilege escalation (IDOR), vertical escalation via role confusion, JWT algorithm confusion attacks, mass assignment.
threats.business_impact: Any user can read/modify any other user's data. Admin panel exposed to regular users. GDPR violation if other users' PII is accessed. A01:2021 — #1 OWASP risk.
mitigations: Enforce authorisation checks server-side for every resource access — never trust client claims.
Implement RBAC/ABAC framework; deny by default.
Use opaque resource IDs (UUIDs) instead of sequential integers to prevent IDOR enumeration.
Validate JWT signature and algorithm server-side; reject 'alg: none'.
Automated DAST scans for authorisation bypass in CI/CD.

threats.title: Broken Access Control — API Gateway

threats.attack_vector: Horizontal privilege escalation (IDOR), vertical escalation via role confusion, JWT algorithm confusion attacks, mass assignment.

threats.title: Identity Spoofing — Auth Service
threats.description: Attacker impersonates a legitimate user or service accessing Auth Service via credential theft, session hijacking, or weak authentication bypass.
threats.attack_vector: Credential stuffing, brute-force, stolen session tokens, OAuth token replay.
threats.business_impact: Unauthorised access to customer accounts or internal systems. Potential for data theft, fraudulent transactions, reputational damage, and regulatory liability under GDPR/PCI-DSS.
mitigations: Enforce MFA on all user-facing authentication flows.
Implement account lockout after 5 failed attempts.
Use short-lived JWTs with refresh token rotation.
Monitor for credential stuffing patterns (IP velocity, geo-anomalies).

threats.title: Sensitive Data Exposure — Auth Service
threats.description: Auth Service stores PII and/or credentials. Exfiltration via unencrypted storage, over-permissive access controls, or API leakage exposes this data.
threats.attack_vector: Direct database access, API response over-sharing, backup file exposure, unencrypted data at rest.
threats.business_impact: GDPR breach notification required within 72 hours. Fines up to €20M or 4% of global turnover. Class action risk. Average PII breach cost: $4.45M (IBM 2023).
mitigations: Encrypt PII at rest using AES-256; rotate encryption keys annually.
Implement field-level encryption for highly sensitive fields (SSN, payment data).
Apply data masking in non-production environments.
Enforce column-level access control — limit which roles can SELECT PII columns.
Audit all queries touching PII tables.

threats.title: Data Tampering — PostgreSQL Database
threats.description: Attacker with excessive DB privileges or via SQLi modifies records in PostgreSQL Database, corrupting financial data, audit trails, or application state.
threats.attack_vector: Overprivileged service accounts, SQLi through upstream app, insider access.
threats.business_impact: Data integrity loss. Corrupted financial records trigger compliance audit failures. Reverting tampered data requires costly forensic investigation.
mitigations: Apply least-privilege — app service account: SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE only, no DDL/DROP.
Enable database audit logging for all DDL and bulk operations.
Implement row-level checksums or audit tables for sensitive records.
Restrict direct DB access to DB admins via PAM jump server.

threats.title: Sensitive Data Exposure — PostgreSQL Database
threats.description: PostgreSQL Database stores PII and/or credentials. Exfiltration via unencrypted storage, over-permissive access controls, or API leakage exposes this data.
threats.attack_vector: Direct database access, API response over-sharing, backup file exposure, unencrypted data at rest.
threats.business_impact: GDPR breach notification required within 72 hours. Fines up to €20M or 4% of global turnover. Class action risk. Average PII breach cost: $4.45M (IBM 2023).
mitigations: Encrypt PII at rest using AES-256; rotate encryption keys annually.
Implement field-level encryption for highly sensitive fields (SSN, payment data).
Apply data masking in non-production environments.
Enforce column-level access control — limit which roles can SELECT PII columns.
Audit all queries touching PII tables.

threats.title: Unencrypted Data Flow — API Gateway → Order Service
threats.description: Data flow 'API Gateway to Order Service' transmits confidential data in plaintext over HTTP. Network-positioned attacker can capture and read all traffic.
threats.attack_vector: Man-in-the-middle attack, network tap, ARP poisoning on internal segments.
threats.business_impact: Full plaintext exposure of all transmitted data. If confidential/restricted: immediate GDPR breach trigger. Credentials in transit = complete system compromise.
mitigations: Upgrade HTTP to TLS 1.2+ minimum; enforce TLS 1.3 where possible.
Implement mutual TLS (mTLS) for service-to-service flows.
Disable HTTP fallback; HSTS with 1-year max-age.

threats.title: Application-Layer DoS — Web App
threats.description: Attacker floods Web App with high-cost requests (complex queries, large uploads, expensive computations) causing resource exhaustion and service unavailability.
threats.attack_vector: HTTP flood, slow-loris, algorithmic complexity attacks, XML/JSON bombs.
threats.business_impact: Service outage. SLA breach — potential penalties. Revenue loss during downtime. Reputational damage to brand.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting per IP and per authenticated user.
Set request size limits and connection timeouts.
Deploy CDN with DDoS protection (Cloudflare/AWS Shield) in front of service.
Implement circuit breakers for downstream dependencies.

threats.title: Missing Audit Trail — PostgreSQL Database
threats.description: PostgreSQL Database does not log critical operations, allowing an attacker (or insider) to perform actions that cannot be attributed or reconstructed.
threats.attack_vector: Any action within the component — no evidence remains after the fact.
threats.business_impact: Incident response is blind. Regulatory fines for inadequate audit trails (GDPR Art. 30, SOC2 CC7). Legal liability if breach cannot be scoped.
mitigations: Implement structured logging (JSON) for all state-changing operations.
Ship logs to centralised SIEM (Splunk/ELK) with tamper-evident storage.
Include user ID, IP, timestamp, action, and resource in every log event.
Set log retention minimum 90 days hot, 1 year cold.

threats.title: Missing Audit Trail — Redis Cache
threats.description: Redis Cache does not log critical operations, allowing an attacker (or insider) to perform actions that cannot be attributed or reconstructed.
threats.attack_vector: Any action within the component — no evidence remains after the fact.
threats.business_impact: Incident response is blind. Regulatory fines for inadequate audit trails (GDPR Art. 30, SOC2 CC7). Legal liability if breach cannot be scoped.
mitigations: Implement structured logging (JSON) for all state-changing operations.
Ship logs to centralised SIEM (Splunk/ELK) with tamper-evident storage.
Include user ID, IP, timestamp, action, and resource in every log event.
Set log retention minimum 90 days hot, 1 year cold.

threats.title: Sensitive Data Exposure — Redis Cache
threats.description: Redis Cache stores credentials. Exfiltration via unencrypted storage, over-permissive access controls, or API leakage exposes this data.
threats.attack_vector: Direct database access, API response over-sharing, backup file exposure, unencrypted data at rest.
threats.business_impact: Credential exposure enables full account takeover and lateral movement across all systems using those credentials.
mitigations: Encrypt PII at rest using AES-256; rotate encryption keys annually.
Implement field-level encryption for highly sensitive fields (SSN, payment data).
Apply data masking in non-production environments.
Enforce column-level access control — limit which roles can SELECT PII columns.
Audit all queries touching PII tables.

threats.title: Unencrypted Data Flow — Order Service → PostgreSQL Database
threats.description: Data flow 'Order Service to Database' transmits confidential data in plaintext over TCP. Network-positioned attacker can capture and read all traffic.
threats.attack_vector: Man-in-the-middle attack, network tap, ARP poisoning on internal segments.
threats.business_impact: Full plaintext exposure of all transmitted data. If confidential/restricted: immediate GDPR breach trigger. Credentials in transit = complete system compromise.
mitigations: Upgrade TCP to TLS 1.2+ minimum; enforce TLS 1.3 where possible.
Implement mutual TLS (mTLS) for service-to-service flows.
Disable HTTP fallback; HSTS with 1-year max-age.

threats.title: Application-Layer DoS — API Gateway
threats.description: Attacker floods API Gateway with high-cost requests (complex queries, large uploads, expensive computations) causing resource exhaustion and service unavailability.
threats.attack_vector: HTTP flood, slow-loris, algorithmic complexity attacks, XML/JSON bombs.
threats.business_impact: Service outage. SLA breach — potential penalties. Revenue loss during downtime. Reputational damage to brand.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting per IP and per authenticated user.
Set request size limits and connection timeouts.
Deploy CDN with DDoS protection (Cloudflare/AWS Shield) in front of service.
Implement circuit breakers for downstream dependencies.

threats.title: Database Query Exhaustion — PostgreSQL Database
threats.description: Unbounded queries or missing connection pooling limits cause PostgreSQL Database connection exhaustion, crashing the database and all dependent services.
threats.attack_vector: N+1 query loops, missing pagination, unrestricted JOIN depth.
threats.business_impact: Full application outage affecting all users. Data writes lost if in-flight transactions are rolled back. Recovery may take hours.
mitigations: Enforce query timeout limits (e.g. 30s max).
Implement connection pooling (PgBouncer/HikariCP) with max pool size.
Add LIMIT clauses to all paginated queries — reject unbounded requests.
Monitor slow query log; alert on queries exceeding 5s.

threats.title: Log Tampering / Evasion — Web App
threats.description: Attacker with access to Web App clears or manipulates logs to cover tracks, undermining forensic investigation and incident response.
threats.attack_vector: Elevated OS/DB access, log rotation exploit, direct log file write access.
threats.business_impact: Incident scope cannot be determined. Forensic investigation fails. Regulatory reporting deadlines missed (72h GDPR breach notification).
mitigations: Forward logs to immutable remote SIEM immediately — local logs are secondary.
Restrict log file write permissions to logging daemon only.
Alert on log gaps, rotation anomalies, or log volume drops.

threats.title: Error Message Information Leakage — Web App
threats.description: Verbose error messages from Web App expose stack traces, internal paths, framework versions, or database query details to unauthenticated users.
threats.attack_vector: Intentional malformed requests; crawler collection of public error pages.
threats.business_impact: Aids attacker reconnaissance — reduces time-to-exploit by revealing exact technology versions with known CVEs.
mitigations: Return generic error messages to clients; log verbose errors server-side only.
Implement global exception handler returning RFC 7807 Problem Details format.
Remove server/framework headers (X-Powered-By, Server).

threats.title: Log Tampering / Evasion — API Gateway
threats.description: Attacker with access to API Gateway clears or manipulates logs to cover tracks, undermining forensic investigation and incident response.
threats.attack_vector: Elevated OS/DB access, log rotation exploit, direct log file write access.
threats.business_impact: Incident scope cannot be determined. Forensic investigation fails. Regulatory reporting deadlines missed (72h GDPR breach notification).
mitigations: Forward logs to immutable remote SIEM immediately — local logs are secondary.

Alert on log gaps, rotation anomalies, or log volume drops.

compliance_flags.description: GDPR requires appropriate technical measures to protect personal data. Critical/High threats against systems processing personal data require immediate remediation.

compliance_flags.requirement: PCI-DSS v4.0 Req. 6 — Develop and Maintain Secure Systems
compliance_flags.description: PCI-DSS requires all Critical/High vulnerabilities to be remediated within 1 month.
